targets.content: Reduction of GHG emissions by 25% by the end of 2030 relative to 1990 base year, subject to significant additional international investments and significant grant assistance; access to an international technology transfer mechanism; co_x0002_financing and participation in international research projects, development of promising low-carbon technologies and initiatives to build local expertise.
Reduction of GHG emissions by 15% by the end of 2030 relative to 1990 base year.

long-term goals are being set, such as reducing the energy intensity of GDP from the 2008 level by 50% by 2050, and increasing the share of alternative sources in electricity generation to 50% by 2050.
The Republic of Kazakhstan aims to achieve carbon neutrality by 2060
The key goal of the Strategy is to make Kazakhstan’s economy resilient to climate change and achieve carbon neutrality by 2060

The mid-term aim of the Strategy (according to the country’s NDC) is to reduce GHG emissions by 2030 by 15% relative to 1990 emissions (unconditional aim) and achieve reduction by 25% subject to international support to decarbonization of the economy (conditional aim).

The mid-term aim of the Strategy (according to the country’s NDC) is to reduce GHG emissions by 2030 by 15% relative to 1990 emissions (unconditional aim) and achieve reduction by 25% subject to international support to decarbonization of the economy (conditional aim).

The NDC raises Kazakhstan’s mitigation target, with national net greenhouse gas emissions set to decline by 17 percent below 1990 levels by 2035 [~328 MtCO₂e in 2030 (15%) and ~320 MtCO₂e (17%) by 2035] (unconditional) and up to 25 percent with international support [~288 MtCO₂e by 2035].

The NDC raises Kazakhstan’s mitigation target, with national net greenhouse gas emissions set to decline by 17 percent below 1990 levels by 2035 [~328 MtCO₂e in 2030 (15%) and ~320 MtCO₂e (17%) by 2035] (unconditional) and up to 25 percent with international support [~288 MtCO₂e by 2035].
long-term goal of achieving carbon neutrality by 2060
The aviation industry will use SAF, of which production in Kazakhstan will reach at least 30.000 tons by 2035.
8.11% below 1990 levels by 2035, a reduction of 2.11 million tonnes CO2eq

measures.instrument: General transport and land use planning
measures.quote: development of sustainable transport
infrastructure for electric and gas vehicles
infrastructure for electric and gas vehicles
smart traffic management systems

measures.instrument: General transport and land use planning
measures.quote: Urban planning and transport infrastructure will be improved to achieve decarbonization in the transport sector.
Sustainable urban mobility and public transport will be promoted through optimization of passenger operations, extensive electrification and gasification
Sustainable urban mobility and public transport will be promoted through optimization of passenger operations, extensive electrification and gasification
Electrification of railways and optimization of freight traffic will continue.
Electrification of railways and optimization of freight traffic will continue.
Shift to transport which uses alternative and renewable energy sources will be encouraged through creation of the respective infrastructure and other incentives.
Shift to transport which uses alternative and renewable energy sources will be encouraged through creation of the respective infrastructure and other incentives.
Development of local manufacturing of vehicles using alternative and renewable energy sources will play an important role in decarbonization of transport
Development of local manufacturing of vehicles using alternative and renewable energy sources will play an important role in decarbonization of transport
Other approaches from international practice of transport decarbonization will be used as well.

International standards will be introduced to enhance investment attractiveness, including standards related to green building, green transport, energy efficiency of buildings and dwellings and environmental management.
Biofuel and hydrogen can be used in modes of transport that are difficult or impossible to fully electrify (e.g. water and air transport).
Biofuel and hydrogen can be used in modes of transport that are difficult or impossible to fully electrify (e.g. water and air transport).
Transport lowers emissions through faster electrification and shifts to public and active mobility.
Transport lowers emissions through faster electrification and shifts to public and active mobility.
Transport lowers emissions through faster electrification and shifts to public and active mobility.

use of transition fuels will be carefully balanced to support cleaner heating - reducing particulate matter and black-carbon emissions - while enhancing power-system flexibility to integrate renewable energy and meet growing demand from the transport and industrial sectors

use of transition fuels will be carefully balanced to support cleaner heating - reducing particulate matter and black-carbon emissions - while enhancing power-system flexibility to integrate renewable energy and meet growing demand from the transport and industrial sectors

Efficiency of energy use will improve through Smart Grid and Smart Metering technologies, reactive power compensation, and the transition to higher voltage levels to support electrification and integration of electric vehicles.
Scale-up of incentives to support uptake of electric cars, together with measures to reduce up-front costs.
The implementation of Euro 5 and Euro 6 standards, as well as an increase in the share of electric vehicles, will significantly improve air quality and reduce pollutant emissions in cities.

Modernization of distribution networks and the transition of consumers to higher voltage will create conditions for the safe connection of charging stations and an increase in the share of electric vehicles, reducing the risk of overload during peak demand hours.
Low-emission fuels will be developed for internal combustion engine-powered vehicles and aviation.
Specifically, plans are underway to use organic additives for gasoline and diesel fuel, for which an additional 70,000 tons of bioethanol will be produced.
The aviation industry will use SAF, of which production in Kazakhstan will reach at least 30,000 tons by 2035.
The development of public transport, rail service, and active mobility will gradually reduce dependence on private cars, improve transport accessibility, and reduce emissions in the sector.
The development of public transport, rail service, and active mobility will gradually reduce dependence on private cars, improve transport accessibility, and reduce emissions in the sector.

Urban planning will be aimed at creating a low-emission transport environment by integrating pedestrian, bicycle, and public transport infrastructure into the urban fabric, ensuring convenience, safety, and sustainability of urban mobility.
Since 2018, provided leasing finance under its vehicle programme to support domestic electric mobility.

adaptation.quote: The 2021-2030 Action Plan contains several measures linked to adaptation to climate change, […] development of sustainable transport

Infrastructure investment in improving resilience to climate change of roads can mitigate the impact of extreme precipitation and floods, while shorter transit time due road quality can reduce transport costs.

Planning and implementation of climate adaptation for low-carbon development will be institutionalized, which includes expansion of the control of government organizations and establishment of mechanisms for financing adaptation as part of national planning and budgeting processes.

The system to collect and distribute relevant and accessible climate data, including data on physical and economic damages due to climate change, will be improved. The data collection system will enable the assessment of risks and vulnerabilities in different sectors and facilitate decision-making at different levels.

Development and implementation of unified national rules for all regions on population evacuation (routes, transport, temporary accommodation) and protection of critical infrastructure facilities (energy, water supply, transport) in the context of climate-induced emergencies

Development and implementation of unified national rules for all regions on population evacuation (routes, transport, temporary accommodation) and protection of critical infrastructure facilities (energy, water supply, transport) in the context of climate-induced emergencies

benefits.quote: The implementation of Euro 5 and Euro 6 standards, as well as an increase in the share of electric vehicles, will significantly improve air quality and reduce pollutant emissions in cities.
The development of public transport, rail service, and active mobility will gradually reduce dependence on private cars, improve transport accessibility, and reduce emissions in the sector.
The development of public transport, rail service, and active mobility will gradually reduce dependence on private cars, improve transport accessibility, and reduce emissions in the sector.

Urban planning will be aimed at creating a low-emission transport environment by integrating pedestrian, bicycle, and public transport infrastructure into the urban fabric, ensuring convenience, safety, and sustainability of urban mobility.
developing public transportation will reduce urban air pollution
